steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content:
The first sentence calls a quad axel an "extreme feat." The blank introduces the second sentence, which describes the audience being left "awestruck" when the first quad axel was landed in competition. The relationship is one of appropriateness: an awestruck crowd is the fitting reaction to an extreme athletic achievement. The transition must signal that the reaction suits the difficulty.
  • Choice A — 'Moreover,': Signals that new, additional information is being layered on. But the crowd's reaction isn't a separate fact being added—it's the appropriate consequence of the feat's difficulty. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Fittingly,': Means "appropriately"—signals that what follows is the suitable or expected outcome of what came before. An awestruck audience is the appropriate response to an extreme feat. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Next,': Marks a step in a sequence or process. The audience's reaction isn't the next step in performing a quad axel—it's an emotional response to the achievement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'However,': Signals contrast or exception. An awestruck reaction does not contrast with a difficult jump; it aligns with it. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: Only "Fittingly" expresses that the awestruck reaction is the appropriate—indeed expected—response to completing the most difficult quad jump. That matches choice B — B is correct.
